people of Kamboja, China, the Yavanas, the Vahlikas, surgeons,
people living on the banks of the Indus, in the Anarta country,
the Pauijdras (residents of Pundra), physicians and the Kiratas
(hunters) will come to grief. But there will be plenty of food and
happiness.
[Our author states succinctly in one verse the effects of eclip-
ses taking place in several lunar months in his WTT^mf^cTr thus—
The metre is ^crfc^rerr. Next he speaks of the ten kinds of the end
of eclipses. See also verse 43 supra.]
55ftemgSr*T fgfe ^ ^ i

Bfhat Samhitn
The end of an eclipse, solar or lunar, is of ten varieties :
(i) Dakfina hanu, (ii) Vdma ham, (iii) Daksinakuk^i, (iv) Vdmakuksi,
(v) Daksinapsyu, (vi) Vamapdyu, (vii) Sahchardana (viii) Jarana, (ix)
Madhyavidarana, and (x) Antayidarana,
[The last is also called Antyavidarana. The definitions and
effects of these ten kinds of Mok^a or release are given in the fol-
lowing nine verses.]
